commands.review_summary:
Adds a 950-line draft RFC defining Model Harness Profiles, profile resolution, migration boundaries, ClawHub registry phases, and verification requirements.

Reproducibility: not applicable. This PR is an RFC-only design proposal, not a bug report with runtime behavior to reproduce.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] This MEMBER-authored draft RFC needs maintainer discussion and acceptance decisions before any automated repair lane would be appropriate.

target.title: RFC: Model Harness Profiles
target.body:
**Status: In progress.** A manual pass over the full RFC is still pending after the Schemator-guided naming update.

## Summary

- Proposes RFC 0009, Model Harness Profiles.
- Replaces the binary local-model Lean direction with a versioned, deterministic
  profile registry.
- Separates model identity, portable harness policy, provider drivers, and
  future managed-local Serving Presets.
- Defines phase-one migration for Lean, GPT-5 response-style settings, and Claude/Opus
  thinking defaults, plus hosted-provider KV/cache non-regression boundaries.

## Scope

This is an RFC only. It does not change OpenClaw runtime behavior or add a
configuration surface in this PR.

## Verification

- `git diff --check`
- Manual review of RFC metadata, required sections, terminology, migration
  inventory, phase boundaries, and privacy-safe content.

## RFC Lifecycle

- Status: draft
- Implementation issue: not created
- Maintainer discussion thread: pending
target.author: vincentkoc
